symbols: lily — purity and the Annunciation tradition
Mary shrinking back — human awe before the divine
white garments — purity and heavenly origin
description: A Pre-Raphaelite Annunciation showing Mary cowering on a narrow bed against a white wall, while a barefoot angel with a flame-like halo offers her a white lily. Both figures wear white garments. The palette is predominantly white with accents of blue and gold.
theological_description: Rossetti captures the terror of the divine encounter. Mary 'was greatly troubled at the saying' (LUK.1.29). Gabriel must reassure her: 'Do not be afraid, Mary' (LUK.1.30). The confined space emphasizes that the Incarnation enters the most ordinary human setting. Her fiat — 'let it be to me according to your word' (LUK.1.38) — is the hinge of salvation.
